Today is February 16, 2026. According to satellite imagery confirmed by BBC Verify, what is the specific location of the US aircraft carrier USS Abraham Lincoln in relation to the coast of Oman?
(A) Approximately 150 miles (240km) off the coast
(B) Exactly 700km from the coastline
(C) Positioned at the Bahrain naval station in the Gulf
(D) Stationed in the eastern Mediterranean near Souda Bay
(E) Approximately 150 kilometers off the coast
explanation: The article states that European Sentinel-2 satellite images show the Abraham Lincoln in the Arabian Sea "around 150 miles (240km) off the coast of Oman."
model_output:
As of February 16, 2026, the US aircraft carrier USS Abraham Lincoln is approximately 150 kilometers (93 miles) off the coast of Oman. This distance is roughly equivalent to 150 miles. Therefore, the correct answer is (E) Approximately 150 kilometers off the coast.

<final_answer>
(E)
</final_answer>

Today is February 16, 2026. Following the death of actor Robert Duvall at age 95, where did his PR agency state that he passed away?
(A) At a medical facility in Los Angeles, California
(B) At his home in Middleburg, Virginia
(C) On the set of a new film project in Texas
(D) At a private estate in the foothills of the Rocky Mountains
(E) In a hospice facility in New York City
explanation: The article specifies that the Oscar-winner died "peacefully at his home in Middleburg, Virginia on Sunday."
model_output:
Robert Duvall, the esteemed actor known for his roles in "The Godfather" and "Apocalypse Now," passed away at the age of 95. According to his publicist and a statement from his wife, Luciana Duvall, he died peacefully at his home in Middleburg, Virginia, on Sunday, February 15, 2026. ([wsls.com](https://www.wsls.com/entertainment/2026/02/16/robert-duvall-oscar-winning-actor-and-godfather-mainstay-dead-at-95/?utm_source=openai))

Therefore, the correct answer is:

<final_answer>
(B)
</final_answer>

(E) 24 years old

model_output:
Sophie Sumner was born on January 15, 1990, in Oxford, England. She won Cycle 18 of America's Next Top Model, which aired from February 29 to May 30, 2012. At that time, she was 22 years old. ([en.wikipedia.org](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sophie_Sumner?utm_source=openai))

Today is February 16, 2026. Former President Barack Obama recently clarified his comments regarding the existence of aliens. What reason did he give for his initial statement that "they're real"?
(A) He was referring to classified documents he read in 2021
(B) He was discussing statistical probabilities of life in the vast universe
(C) He was attempting to confirm the existence of an underground facility in Area 51
(D) He was trying to stick with the spirit of the speed round of questions
(E) He was referencing unexplained footage of objects in the sky from 2009
explanation: Obama explained that his initial comments were made "in line with 'the spirit of the speed round' of questions" during the podcast interview.
model_output:
Former President Barack Obama recently addressed speculation about extraterrestrial life following a lighthearted comment he made during a podcast with host Brian Tyler Cohen. When asked if aliens are real, Obama responded affirmatively but clarified later on Instagram that he had not seen any evidence of alien contact during his presidency. He explained that although the vastness of the universe suggests the probability of life elsewhere, the immense distances between solar systems make it unlikely that aliens have visited Earth. He also debunked ongoing conspiracy theories about Area 51, reiterating that contrary to popular belief, it does not house extraterrestrials. ([apnews.com](https://apnews.com/article/a23f03ebb1b4c3009415b20bec3df26b?utm_source=openai))

In his clarification, Obama stated, "I was trying to stick with the spirit of the speed round, but since it's gotten attention, let me clarify. Statistically, the universe is so vast that the odds are good there's life out there. But the distances between solar systems are so great that the chances we've ... !" ([pbs.org](https://www.pbs.org/newshour/science/obama-shuts-down-alien-buzz-no-evidence-during-my-presidency?utm_source=openai))

Therefore, the reason for his initial statement that "they're real" was that he was attempting to stick with the spirit of ...  of questions.

<final_answer>
(D)
</final_answer>

Today is February 16, 2026. In the ongoing investigation into the disappearance of Nancy Guthrie, what specific physical description did the FBI provide for the suspect seen on doorbell-camera footage?
(A) Approximately 6ft tall with a heavy build
(B) Between 5ft 7in and 5ft 8in tall with a slim build
(C) Exactly 5ft 11in tall with an athletic build
(D) Under 5ft 6in tall with a stocky build
(E) Between 5ft 9in and 5ft 10in tall with an average build
explanation: The FBI announced that based on forensic analysis of the video, they believe the man was "between 5ft 9in and 5ft 10in (175-177cm) tall with an average build."
model_output:
The FBI has provided the following physical description of the suspect seen on the doorbell-camera footage in the investigation into Nancy Guthrie's disappearance:

- **Height**: Approximately 5 feet 9 inches to 5 feet 10 inches tall
- **Build**: Average
- **Additional Details**: The individual was carrying a black, 25-liter Ozark Trail Hiker Pack backpack. ([cbsnews.com](https://www.cbsnews.com/news/nancy-guthrie-case-fbi-releases-first-description-of-suspect-increases-reward-to-100000-dollars/?utm_source=openai))

Based on this description, the correct answer is:

<final_answer>
(E)
</final_answer>

===============================================

Today is February 16, 2026. According to NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte, what is the projected German defense budget by the year 2029?
(A) €150bn
(B) €100bn
(C) €200bn
(D) €75bn
(E) €125bn
explanation: Mark Rutte pointed out that by 2029, the German defense budget will be "€150bn," which he described as a "staggering amount."
model_output:
NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte has stated that Germany plans to increase its defense spending to 3.5% of its GDP by 2029, which, combined with an additional 1.5% for security-related investments, will bring the total defense expenditure to 5% of GDP by that year. ([nato.int](https://www.nato.int/en/news-and-events/events/transcripts/2025/12/11/joint-press-conference-by-nato-secretary-general-mark-rutte-with-german-federal-chancellor-friedrich-merz?utm_source=openai)) This commitment represents a significant increase from Germany's previous defense budget. In 2021, Germany's defense spending was approximately €70 billion, and by 2029, it is projected to reach about €150 billion, more than doubling the 2021 figure. ([mundoamerica.com](https://www.mundoamerica.com/news/2025/06/24/685a841fe9cf4a01348b458f.html?utm_source=openai))

Therefore, the projected German defense budget by 2029 is €150 billion.

<final_answer>
(C)
</final_answer>

explanation: The article states: "residents are reeling after a mass shooting left nine people dead, including six children."

- **Abel Mwansa**, 12 years old
- **Ezekiel Schofield**, 13 years old
- **Kylie Smith**, 12 years old
- **Zoey Benoit**, 12 years old
- **Ticaria Lampert**, 12 years old

- **Jennifer Jacobs**, 39 years old
- **Emmett Jacobs**, 11 years old
- **Jesse Van Rootselaar**, 18 years old (perpetrator)

Therefore, the number of children among the nine total victims is five.

<final_answer>
(E)

Today is February 16, 2026. According to White House claims, how much will reversing the "endangerment finding" reduce costs for automobile manufacturers per car?
(A) $1.3 trillion
(B) $7.5 billion
(C) $2,400
(D) $1,760
(E) $20,000
explanation: The article states: "Reversing the finding will reduce automobile manufacturers' costs by around $2,400 (£1,760) per car, the White House claimed."
model_output:
The "endangerment finding" refers to the 2009 EPA determination that greenhouse gas emissions from vehicles pose a threat to public health and welfare, leading to regulations on vehicle emissions. The Trump administration's proposal to rescind this finding aims to eliminate these regulations. According to the White House, reversing the "endangerment finding" would reduce costs for automobile manufacturers by an average of $2,400 per vehicle. ([climatedepot.com](https://www.climatedepot.com/2026/02/13/white-house-says-it-will-save-americans-2400-on-car-purchases-start-stop-feature-on-vehicles-will-no-longer-be-a-requirement/?utm_source=openai))

Therefore, the correct answer is (C) $2,400.

<final_answer>
(C)
</final_answer>

Today is February 16, 2026. The Environmental Defense Fund estimates that by 2055, the increase in emissions could lead to how many premature deaths?
(A) 7.5 to 18 billion
(B) 10 million
(C) 15,400 to 58,000
(D) 1.3 trillion
(E) 2,400 to 5,000
explanation: The article states: "The Environmental Defense Fund estimates that, by 2055, the increase in emissions could lead to between 15,400 and 58,000 premature deaths."
model_output:
The Environmental Defense Fund (EDF) has projected that by 2055, the increase in emissions could lead to approximately 41,400 premature deaths in the United States. ([edf.org](https://www.edf.org/media/new-analysis-epa-clean-vehicle-standards-will-save-thousands-lives-protect-health-slash-air?utm_source=openai)) This figure aligns with option (C), which estimates between 15,400 and 58,000 premature deaths.

The other options are significantly higher than this estimate:

- Option (A) suggests 7.5 to 18 billion premature deaths, which is an astronomically high number and not supported by any credible sources.

- Option (B) estimates 10 million premature deaths, which is also much higher than the EDF's projection.

- Option (D) suggests 1.3 trillion premature deaths, an impossibly large number.

- Option (E) estimates 2,400 to 5,000 premature deaths, which is lower than the EDF's projection.

Therefore, the most accurate estimate based on EDF's projection is option (C).

<final_answer>
(C)
</final_answer>

(D) Jordan

explanation: The article states: "...we have seen an increase of F-15 and EA-18 fighter jets stationed in Muwaffaq Salti military base in Jordan..."
model_output:
The Muwaffaq Salti Air Base, located in Al Azraq, Jordan, has recently seen an increase in U.S. fighter jets. Satellite imagery from early February 2026 revealed the presence of 17 F-15E Strike Eagle fighter jets, 8 A-10 Thunderbolt attack aircraft, 4 C-130 transport aircraft, and 4 helicopters at the base. Notably, 4 EA-18G Growler electronic warfare aircraft were also detected, equipment that was not visible in images taken at the end of January. ([baonghean.vn](https://baonghean.vn/en/my-tang-cuong-he-thong-patriot-va-may-bay-chien-dau-tai-cac-can-cu-trung-dong-10323437.html?utm_source=openai))

This deployment is part of a broader U.S. military buildup in the Middle East, aimed at enhancing regional security and stability amid escalating tensions. The increased presence at Muwaffaq Salti Air Base underscores its strategic importance in U.S. operations within the region.

<final_answer>
(D)
</final_answer>
